title: dracaena serrulata subsp. dhofarica
intro: Dracaena serrulata subsp. dhofarica, also known as the Dhofar dragon tree, is a plant species belonging to the Dracaena genus, native to the Dhofar region in Oman. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Arabic medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing pain and promoting wound healing. In terms of herbal preparations, Dracaena serrulata subsp. dhofarica can be made into a tea or tincture, which is then used to treat various health issues.
